MONET 0.1.0
  • Reference

MONET: Log ratio of Ancestral Variances

Source: R/MONET-package.R
MONET-package.Rd

The MONET package provides tools for identifying potential patterns of local adaptation. It does so by following the method LogAV, which estimates ancestral variances through two different equations, one using within-population coancestry, and another using between-population coancestry. MONET has a function to calculate coancestry matrices which are necessary for estimating the ancestral variances.

The MONET package provides tools for analyzing the log ratio of ancestral variances. This package helps researchers analyze genomic data and evolutionary patterns through statistical methods specialized for variance analysis.

Main Functions

The MONET package provides several key functions:

  • monet: Main function for estimating log ratios of ancestral variances

  • calculate_coancestries: Calculate coancestry matrices from genetic data

  • kinship_from_pedigree: Calculate kinship matrix from pedigree data

  • counting_blocks_matrix: Utility function for matrix block counting

Dependencies

This package relies on:

  • brms: For Bayesian mixed-effects modeling

  • hierfstat: For population genetics calculations

References

Goudet & Weir (2023), do O et al (2025)

See also

Useful links:

  • https://github.com/isadoo/MONET

  • https://isadoo.github.io/MONET/
